slides.title: Workflow JSON IR
slides.headline: Promote Blok's v2 workflow shape to one published, versioned, canvas-aware JSON IR
slides.beforeCaption: No version stamp, no canvas slot; VS Code validates it against a stale hand-written schema, Studio against nothing.
slides.afterCaption: Same file plus a semver discriminant and a round-trippable ui slot, validated everywhere by one generated schema.
slides.why: One generated, addressable schema lets LLMs constrain-decode and self-validate, the canvas round-trips layout, and three-way schema drift becomes structurally impossible.
slides.compat: Non-breaking; schemaVersion defaults to "2" and ui is optional

slides.title: Versioned Node IDs
slides.headline: Pin a node by scope and version right inside the step's use: ref
slides.beforeCaption: No version, no scope guarantee — last month's green run can drift on upgrade.
slides.afterCaption: Mandatory scope kills typosquatting; the version pin travels in the ref itself.
slides.why: Gives AI a trustworthy, scope-namespaced, version-pinned install identity — the foundation the registry (S6) and multi-runtime packaging (S9) build on.
slides.compat: Opt-in via schema 2; v1 files run byte-for-byte unchanged

slides.title: Conditions Just Work
slides.headline: Route branch and loop conditions through the same Mapper that step inputs already use.
slides.beforeCaption: The obvious condition silently 500s; only eq() is safe.
slides.afterCaption: One resolver for inputs, branch, loop — the obvious form is correct.
slides.why: Kills a live silent-500 that every AI- and canvas-generated condition would reproduce; one mental model, plus a compiled-Function cache speeding large forEach/loop.
slides.compat: Non-breaking; pure bug-fix + perf behind existing BLOK_MAPPER_MODE

slides.title: One Module Contract
slides.headline: Extract one generic module-descriptor contract so triggers, runtimes, and nodes stop forking observability's code.
slides.beforeCaption: One union per surface; a cyclic dep freezes the whole CLI.
slides.afterCaption: One string-keyed contract; one Set turns the freeze into a clear error.
slides.why: S8 triggers and node-install become half-day data files, not subtree forks; the cycle hang and a transactional-apply bug get fixed once at the root.
slides.compat: Non-breaking — internal CLI plumbing, observability behavior byte-identical

slides.title: Editable Studio
slides.headline: Studio gains a write path: edit workflows on the canvas, not just watch them run.
slides.beforeCaption: You can see the graph but can't change a single step.
slides.afterCaption: Drag, insert, rename, save — the canvas is the editor now.
slides.why: Closes the see-edit-save loop in the browser; AI and humans edit the same JSON IR through one shared validator the runner already trusts.
slides.compat: Non-breaking: new route, ephemeral positions, TS workflows stay read-only

slides.title: Connect-Picker
slides.headline: Click an upstream output field instead of hand-typing $.state references that silently break
slides.beforeCaption: Author guesses the upstream id and output path blind
slides.afterCaption: Picker emits a provably-valid reference, previewed before save
slides.why: Kills the biggest silent-pass-through bug class: hand-typed references become one click, type-checked against schema and the live trace store before runtime.
slides.compat: Non-breaking; additive endpoints + Studio UI, zero IR change

slides.title: The Blok Registry
slides.headline: A Blok-owned registry that resolves, integrity-checks, and provenances every node before an AI installs it.
slides.beforeCode:
$ blokctl install stripe-charge

# package-manager.ts:70 — no version pin, floats to latest
INSTALL_NODE: (opts) => `npm install ${opts.node}`
#   → npm install @acme/stripe-charge   (whatever "latest" is)
#   → npm runs the artifact's postinstall script
#   → regex-patches src/Nodes.ts to register it
# No scope guarantee, no immutability, no integrity hash,
# no provenance, npm-only (Go/Python nodes can't ship).
slides.beforeCaption: Unpinned `npm install` — the AI's trust boundary is npm's reactive posture.
slides.afterCode:
$ blokctl install @acme/stripe-charge@^2.0.0

# Resolve against static Blok packument (CDN, no DB)
GET /@acme/stripe-charge        → versions, dist-tags
# Pick runtime-matched artifact, verify Blok-computed SRI
#   integrity: "sha512-…"   provenance: Rekor #123456
# Publish gate already rejected: install scripts, version
#   reuse, unowned scope.  blok.lock pins (ref, ver, hash).
slides.afterCaption: Scoped, pinned, hashed, provenanced — installable unattended by an AI.
slides.why: An autonomous AI can install nodes safely: mandatory scopes kill typosquatting, zero-install-scripts neutralizes npm's #1 malware vector, and integrity plus provenance make installs reproducible.
slides.compat: Non-breaking — versionless refs resolve to `latest`; pinned scoped refs are opt-in

slides.title: Modular Triggers
slides.headline: Add and remove triggers with one CLI command instead of hand-wiring framework internals.
slides.beforeCaption: Triggers are hardwired surgery; pure-HTTP installs drag in 4 unused packages.
slides.afterCode:
# Lean by default — http ships alone
blokctl create project --triggers http

# Opt in to a webhook receiver, one command:
blokctl trigger add webhook   # installs pkg, deps,
                              # regenerates the mounted App

blokctl trigger list --json   # machine-readable inventory
blokctl trigger remove grpc   # drops pkg + config + port
slides.afterCaption: One command installs, wires, and inventories a trigger — siblings become opt-in.
slides.why: Pure-HTTP projects stay lean, removal is real, and an AI agent can assemble trigger infra by command — mirroring the shipped `blokctl observability` pattern.
slides.compat: Non-breaking; lean App ships only in new scaffolds, existing projects boot unchanged

slides.title: Multi-Runtime Nodes
slides.headline: One node identity, N runtime implementations, installed and pinned by version instead of regex-patched.
slides.beforeCaption: One npm tarball, hand-patched into Nodes.ts as a class instance — JS-only and unpinnable.
slides.afterCaption: Same use: ref resolves to the runtime you enabled; blok.lock pins exact (version, hash).
slides.why: An AI can resolve, install, and trust a node by (scope, name, version, hash) — and Blok's 8-runtime differentiator finally shows up in the product.
slides.compat: Non-breaking; version pins opt-in, versionless use: refs unchanged

slides.title: Managed Connections
slides.headline: Nodes declare a connection; the runner injects the secret — never the workflow artifact
slides.beforeCaption: Secret lives in ctx.env (default-open) — no reuse, no AI-wireable auth contract
slides.afterCaption: Artifact carries a name, not sk_live_…; secret stays off ctx.env and the trace
slides.why: Unlocks the connector marketplace: connect once, reuse across 40 steps, rotate in one place, and let an MCP agent wire auth autonomously.
slides.compat: Non-breaking — both fields optional, no IR version bump

slides.title: AI-Native Surface
slides.headline: Author errors enumerate the real step ids, so the AI self-corrects in one shot.
slides.beforeCaption: Generic hint never names the steps that DO exist — the AI loops.
slides.afterCaption: Error names the exact available ids — the AI fixes it immediately.
slides.why: Turns Blok's Zod schemas, JSON IR, and test harness into a self-correcting agent loop — the AI authors a backend in a day, not a debugging spiral.
slides.compat: Additive; behavior change gated by BLOK_MAPPER_MODE

slides.title: Marketplace Trust
slides.headline: Replace ungated `npm publish` with a server-side gate that auto-assigns a trust tier
slides.beforeCaption: No floor, no provenance, no tier — an AI installs a typosquat blind
slides.afterCaption: community floor + automated `verified` badge an agent can gate on
slides.why: Lets an AI install on its own — safely. Mechanical tiers, OIDC tokens, zero install scripts; no human review queue, no relicensing time-bomb.
slides.compat: Purely additive — existing nodes default to community; core stays Apache-2.0
